goal: Determine whether this project should be rewritten in Rust and what the safest Rust adoption path would be.
deliverable: A feasibility assessment for Rust rewrite options
A lower-risk migration path if Rust is still desired
constraints: Base the analysis on current repo architecture and dependencies
Do not implement any Rust code in this turn
Separate technical possibility from current strategic fit
acceptance_criteria: The analysis covers OCR, backend, desktop, schema, release workflow, and frontend coupling
The output gives a clear recommendation
The report includes an incremental path rather than only a yes/no answer
product_acceptance_criteria: The user can decide whether Rust is worth pursuing now
The team can see whether a full rewrite or hybrid path fits the current product stage better
manual_spot_checks: Check Python-only dependencies and OCR stack
Check desktop shell and packaging model
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the rewrite assessment is written, evidence-backed, and clearly bounded as analysis-only.
delivery_truth_contract: Report current repo facts and explicit engineering judgment without claiming any Rust migration exists yet.
non_goals: Creating a detailed full rewrite task graph
inferred_assumptions: The user is considering Rust as a serious migration option.
OCR/document parsing and Windows desktop delivery remain non-negotiable product capabilities.
The best answer should optimize for delivery realism rather than language enthusiasm.
